blocks.text:
This deck is built with Vela. Every slide demonstrates the feature it describes.
Click **🤖 Vera** to start editing — this is your playground.
Vela is a 6,600-line React app that runs as a Claude.ai artifact. You describe your deck — Vera builds it.
This flow block you just saw? One of 22+ semantic block types. No text boxes — structured content.

items: Design is a separate, manual step
Live AI editing inside the deck

blocks.text: ↑ This comparison uses a grid block — 2 columns, each with its own heading + bullets.
↑ Four metric blocks in a grid. Each has value, label, icon, and optional trend indicator.
Every text element supports **bold** and *italic* markdown. Sizes range from xs to 4xl.
Callouts draw attention to key insights. Icon, border, and background are customizable.
Process visualization with semantic connectors, roadmaps, and sequences.
Icon rows replace bullet lists with visual impact. Grids structure any content into columns.
Lucide icon set with PascalCase names
Dark, midnight, light, warm, vibrant, editorial, minimal
Custom vector graphics with theme tokens
For diagrams that structured blocks can't express — meshes, fan-outs, custom layouts.

blocks.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 500 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='40' width='100' height='60' rx='8' fill='none' stroke='{{accent}}' stroke-width='2'/><text x='60' y='75' text-anchor='middle' fill='{{color}}' font-size='13' font-family='monospace'>Skill</text><rect x='150' y='40' width='100' height='60' rx='8' fill='none' stroke='{{accent}}' stroke-width='2'/><text x='200' y='75' text-anchor='middle' fill='{{color}}' font-size='13' font-family='monospace'>Claude</text><rect x='290' y='40' width='100' height='60' rx='8' fill='none' stroke='{{accent}}' stroke-width='2'/><text x='340' y='75' text-anchor='middle' fill='{{color}}' font-size='13' font-family='monospace'>Artifact</text><rect x='430' y='40' width='60' height='60' rx='8' fill='{{accent}}' fill-opacity='0.15' stroke='{{accent}}' stroke-width='2'/><text x='460' y='75' text-anchor='middle' fill='{{accent}}' font-size='13' font-family='monospace'>You</text><line x1='110' y1='70' x2='150' y2='70' stroke='{{muted}}' stroke-width='1.5' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><line x1='250' y1='70' x2='290' y2='70' stroke='{{muted}}' stroke-width='1.5' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><line x1='390' y1='70' x2='430' y2='70' stroke='{{muted}}' stroke-width='1.5' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><defs><marker id='arr' viewBox='0 0 10 10' refX='9' refY='5' markerWidth='6' markerHeight='6' orient='auto'><path d='M 0 0 L 10 5 L 0 10 z' fill='{{muted}}'/></marker></defs></svg>
blocks.caption: Vela pipeline: Skill → Claude → Artifact → You

blocks.text: SVG blocks use {{accent}}, {{color}}, {{muted}}, {{bg}} tokens — they adapt to any theme automatically.
An agentic AI assistant *inside* the artifact. Vera has 18 tools for deck manipulation — she builds, edits, searches, and restyles without leaving the canvas.

Click the **🤖 Vera** button on the right to open the chat panel.
Try the exercises on the next slides — all of them work on *this* deck.

items.title: Open Vera
items.text: Click the 🤖 Vera button in the top bar

items.title: Restyle command
items.text: Type: "Restyle all slides to a warm editorial theme with amber accents"

items.title: Watch it work
items.text: Vera will use batch_restyle to update every slide in the deck

items.title: Compare
items.text: Ctrl+Z to undo and compare before/after

blocks.text: Try other themes: midnight violet, vibrant startup, minimal corporate, warm light.

items.title: Choose a topic
items.text: Something you know well — your product, a hobby, a concept

items.title: Ask Vera
items.text: Type: "Add a 3-slide section on [your topic] with a flow diagram and metrics"

items.title: Navigate
items.text: New slides appear in the left panel — click to view

items.title: Edit inline
items.text: Click any text on the slide to edit it directly

blocks.text: Vera uses add_lane + add_item + add_slide tools. Each tool call is visible as a trace card in the chat.
Try these commands with Vera — each demonstrates a different tool:
Find all slides with flow blocks
Uses find_slides with block_type filter — returns clickable jump links.
Replace Vela with VELA across the whole deck
Uses find_replace — modifies all matching text in-place.
Run deck_stats and show me quality issues
Returns slide count, total time, block distribution, and issues.
Set branding with a red accent bar and My Company footer
Uses set_branding — overlays on every slide.
The left panel organizes your deck into **lanes** and **modules**. Each module holds its slides. Drag to reorder, click to jump.
Press [ to toggle the navigator panel
In fullscreen mode, hover the left edge to reveal a **searchable table of contents**. Type to filter, click to jump across modules.
Works across lanes — navigate the whole deck from one panel
Three levels of AI editing — from single blocks to the entire deck.
Press **E** on any slide. Describe a change, paste an image as reference.
Press **Shift+I** to let Vera analyze and improve the slide with visual context.
Click **🎲 Variants** for 4 different design directions. Preview with **1-4**, accept with **Enter**.
Use the **Batch** button to restyle or improve across module, section, or full deck.
Every AI edit supports undo (Ctrl+Z). Preview alternatives with 1-4 keys, accept with Enter, dismiss with Esc.
Vector-quality canvas rendering. Clickable links preserved. Branding watermark. Supports 16:9, 1:1, and 4:5 ratios.
Full deck exported as structured markdown with headings, bullets, and optional speaker notes. Great for docs or READMEs.
Import/export the raw deck JSON. Copy-paste between decks, version in Git, or re-import into another Vela artifact.
Click any text on a slide to edit it directly. Supports **bold** and *italic* markdown. No modal, no sidebar — just click and type.
The Fit view mode automatically scales slides to fill the available panel width. Switch to 16:9, 1:1, or 4:5 from the view dropdown for fixed ratios.
Reorder slides by dragging thumbnails in the navigator. Move modules between lanes. Reorganize your deck without Vera.
Set a custom accent bar, logo position, and footer text via the Brand panel. Overlays every slide consistently — edit once, apply everywhere.
Inline editing works in fullscreen too — click any text on a slide to fix a typo or tweak wording mid-presentation. No need to exit.
Any text block, bullet, or icon-row item supports a **link** property. Clickable in both edit and present mode — opens in a new tab.
Ctrl+V an image into the Vera chat, the quick edit prompt, or even the new slide dialog. Vera uses it as visual reference for design.

Claude.ai runs artifacts in a sandboxed iframe — **browser fullscreen is blocked by default**. In presenter mode, click the **⛵ icon** (top right) to copy a bookmarklet. Add it to your bookmarks bar once — then click it anytime you present for true fullscreen.

blocks.text:
This deck is your playground. Try the **+ New** button for a fresh canvas,
or keep building on what's here. Vera is ready.

blocks.text: ELv2 — © 2025-present Rui Quintino · Vera AI powered by your Claude.ai subscription

pip install vela-slides
vela deck create my-deck.json
vela deck ship my-deck.json
From install to presentation in under a minute.
Vela transformed how our team creates presentations. What used to take hours now takes minutes.
blocks.attribution: Sarah Chen, VP of Product at TechCorp

blocks.text: Dividers create visual breathing room between content sections.
Use them sparingly to group related ideas and improve readability.
Embed images from any URL with automatic scaling.
Always validate your deck before shipping. Run `vela deck validate` to catch issues early.
Use compact format to reduce token usage by 32% when generating decks with AI.

items: AI-native design — built for Claude.ai artifacts
21 semantic block types for any content
Zero dependencies — pure React, inline styles
CLI tooling for validation, editing, and export

blocks.text: Create stunning presentations with AI in minutes.
